verifiers.output: All required tool call evidence found

judge.verdict: PASS - Completed transition coordination and training communication

dimension_scores.reason: The agent sent an email to david.thompson@amazon.com via email-env__send_email acknowledging his frustration about losing Lisa mid-cycle and thanking him for his patience, professionalism, and continued support during the transition.

A 30-minute transition planning meeting with both David Thompson and Michael Chen as required attendees was created with a clear transition-focused title/description, but it was scheduled for 2025-07-23 rather than during the rubric-specified week of Feb 1-7 2026.

dimension_scores.dimension: Information Gathering via Chat & Training Email to Lisa
dimension_scores.reason: The agent first DM'd Sarah Johnson and Michael Chen, received Sarah's 4 modules and Michael's 2 additional requirements, and only afterward emailed lisa.anderson@amazon.com a combined 6-module list with context about her new Recruiting Coordinator role.

The agent verified contact details from HRIS, updated EMP-006 to designation 'Recruiting Coordinator' and reports_to 'Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager)', and also created a Note documenting the transition.

evidence: email-env__get_email retrieved David Thompson's complaint email describing being blindsided mid-cycle while onboarding 12 new hires, providing the context the acknowledgment should address.

email-env__send_email sent 'Re: Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Thank You for Your Support' to david.thompson@amazon.com, and the body explicitly says it understands his frustration and thanks him for his patience, professionalism, and continued support.

rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm and rocketchat-env__rocketchat_get_room_history show Sarah Johnson provided 4 modules: Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals, Interviewing Best Practices, Diversity Sourcing Strategies, and Offer Letter Processing.

rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm and rocketchat-env__rocketchat_get_room_history show Michael Chen added 2 requirements: ATS Power User Certification and Candidate Experience Standards Training, and provided meeting availability.

email-env__send_email sent Lisa Anderson an email at lisa.anderson@amazon.com listing all 6 training modules, grouped by Sarah and Michael, with context about her reassignment to Recruiting Coordinator in Talent Acquisition.

chronos-server__create_event created a 30-minute event titled 'Transition Planning Meeting — David Thompson & Michael Chen' from 2025-07-23T13:00:00 to 2025-07-23T13:30:00 with both david.thompson@amazon.com and michael.chen@amazon.com as required participants.
A second chronos-server__create_event call created the same meeting on Michael Chen's calendar as well, reinforcing that both calendars reflect the meeting.
frappe-hrms-env__frappe_update_resource updated EMP-006 so Lisa Anderson's designation became 'Recruiting Coordinator' and reports_to became 'Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager)'.
frappe-hrms-env__frappe_create_resource created a Note documenting the reassignment, training requirements, and transition planning, with STATE DIFF showing notes increased from 0 to 1.
failed_criteria: Calendar meeting was not scheduled during the rubric-specified week of Feb 1-7 2026; it was scheduled for July 23, 2025.
notes: The agent went beyond the prompt by DMing David for availability and making HRIS updates; these are helpful but not necessary for full credit.

The email to David largely met the intent, though the explicit phrase thanks for his 'support during the handoff' was expressed more generally as appreciation for patience, professionalism, and continued support during the transition.

artifacts.task: Lisa Anderson is being reassigned from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. David Thompson emailed HR upset about losing her mid-cycle — send him an empathetic acknowledgment email thanking him for his support during the handoff. Schedule a 30-minute transition planning meeting between David Thompson and Michael Chen this week. Before emailing Lisa her required training modules, check with Sarah Johnson via chat for the list of training modules Lisa needs to complete, and also confirm with Michael Chen via chat whether he has any additional onboarding requirements for Lisa's new role. Then email Lisa the complete combined list.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Lisa Anderson is being reassigned from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. David Thompson emailed HR upset about losing her mid-cycle — send him an empathetic acknowledgment email thanking him for his support during the handoff. Schedule a 30-minute transition planning meeting between David Thompson and Michael Chen this week. Before emailing Lisa her required training modules, check with Sarah Johnson via chat for the list of training modules Lisa needs to complete, and also confirm with Michael Chen via chat whether he has any additional onboarding requirements for Lisa's new role. Then email Lisa the complete combined list.
I'll work through this systematically. Let me start with Phase 1: Research.

{"message_id": "msg-fcca65b5fa92", "from": "david.thompson@amazon.com", "to": "hr@amazon.com", "subject": "Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Seriously Frustrated", "body_text": "Hi HR, I just found out that Lisa Anderson is being moved to Talent Acquisition. Frankly, I'm blindsided by this. We're in the middle of onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires and Lisa is the linchpin holding the whole thing together. Pulling her out now is going to derail weeks of planning. I feel like nobody consulted me or even gave me a heads-up. This is incredibly disrespectful to my team and the work we've been doing. I don't understand why these decisions get made without involving the people who are actually impacted. I need to know what the plan is for covering her responsibilities. This can't just be dumped on the rest of us. David Thompson", "body_html": "

Hi HR,

I just found out that Lisa Anderson is being moved to Talent Acquisition. Frankly, I'm blindsided by this. We're in the middle of onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires and Lisa is the linchpin holding the whole thing together. Pulling her out now is going to derail weeks of planning.

I feel like nobody consulted me or even gave me a heads-up. This is incredibly disrespectful to my team and the work we've been doing. I don't understand why these decisions get made without involving the people who are actually impacted.

I need to know what the plan is for covering her responsibilities. This can't just be dumped on the rest of us.

David Thompson

", "timestamp": 1774040583, "cc": "", "bcc": ""}

Now I have the full picture. Let me proceed to Phase 2: Consult stakeholders via DM. I need to DM Sarah Johnson (for training modules), Michael Chen (for additional onboarding requirements), and also David Thompson and Lisa Anderson as they're directly involved.

function.arguments: {"to_username": "sarah_johnson", "text": "Hi Sarah, I'm processing Lisa Anderson's reassignment from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. Could you please share the list of training modules Lisa needs to complete for her new role? I want to make sure I send her the complete list. Thank you!"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments: {"to_username": "michael_chen", "text": "Hi Michael, Lisa Anderson is being reassigned to your Talent Acquisition team as a Recruiting Coordinator. Do you have any additional onboarding requirements or training modules you'd like Lisa to complete as she transitions into her new role under your team? I want to compile a comprehensive list for her. Also, I'm scheduling a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and David Thompson this week to discuss the handoff \u2014 what times work best for you? Thanks!"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments: {"to_username": "david_thompson", "text": "Hi David, I received your email regarding Lisa Anderson's reassignment and I completely understand your frustration. I'm working on this now and want to make sure the transition is as smooth as possible. I'm planning to schedule a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and Michael Chen this week to discuss the handoff and coverage plan. What times work best for you this week? Thank you for your patience and support through this process."}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments:
{"to": "david.thompson@amazon.com", "subject": "Re: Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Thank You for Your Support", "body": "Hi David,

Thank you for reaching out, and I want you to know that your concerns have been heard. I completely understand how frustrating it must be to learn about Lisa's reassignment mid-cycle, especially when your team is in the middle of onboarding 12 new hires and Lisa has been such a critical part of that effort.

Your dedication to your team and the onboarding process is truly appreciated, and I recognize that this transition puts additional pressure on you and your team. Please know that this decision was not made lightly, and I'm sorry that the communication around it didn't happen the way it should have.

To help ensure a smooth handoff, I've scheduled a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and Michael Chen for this Wednesday, July 23, from 1:00–1:30 PM PT. This will be an opportunity to discuss coverage plans, timeline, and how to minimize disruption to your current onboarding cohort.

Thank you for your patience, professionalism, and continued support during this transition. Your leadership through this process means a great deal, and we're committed to making sure your team has the resources it needs.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any additional questions or concerns.

Best regards,
HR Team"}

function.arguments:
{"to": "lisa.anderson@amazon.com", "subject": "Required Training Modules for Your New Role — Recruiting Coordinator, Talent Acquisition", "body": "Hi Lisa,

Congratulations on your reassignment to the Talent Acquisition team as a Recruiting Coordinator! We're excited about this next chapter in your career.

To help you get up to speed in your new role, please complete the following training modules:

From Sarah Johnson (VP of Talent Acquisition):
1. Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
2. Interviewing Best Practices
3. Diversity Sourcing Strategies
4. Offer Letter Processing

From Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager):
5. ATS Power User Certification
6. Candidate Experience Standards Training

Please prioritize completing all six modules prior to your official start in the new role. If you have any questions about the content or need help with enrollment, don't hesitate to reach out.

Welcome to the Talent Acquisition team!

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

Effective Date: July 2025

Reassignment Details:
- Previous Role: Onboarding Specialist, reporting to David Thompson (Onboarding Manager)
- New Role: Recruiting Coordinator, Talent Acquisition, reporting to Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager)

Required Training Modules:
1. Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
2. Interviewing Best Practices
3. Diversity Sourcing Strategies
4. Offer Letter Processing
5. ATS Power User Certification
6. Candidate Experience Standards Training

Transition Planning:
- Transition planning meeting scheduled between David Thompson and Michael Chen on July 23, 2025, 1:00–1:30 PM PT.
- David Thompson's concerns about mid-cycle disruption acknowledged and addressed.

HRIS Updated: Designation changed to Recruiting Coordinator, reports_to updated to Michael Chen."}}

---

## Summary of Completed Actions

### 1. Research & Context
- Read David Thompson's email to HR expressing frustration about Lisa Anderson's mid-cycle reassignment while onboarding 12 new hires.
- Retrieved employee records for all four stakeholders: Lisa Anderson (EMP-006), David Thompson (EMP-005), Michael Chen (EMP-002), and Sarah Johnson (EMP-001).

### 2. Stakeholder Consultations (via RocketChat DMs)
- **Sarah Johnson** — Provided the required training modules: Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals, Interviewing Best Practices, Diversity Sourcing Strategies, and Offer Letter Processing.
- **Michael Chen** — Added two additional requirements: ATS Power User Certification and Candidate Experience Standards Training. Available Wednesday/Thursday 1:00–4:00 PM PT.
- **David Thompson** — Acknowledged his concerns and gathered availability: Wednesday 1:00–4:00 PM or Friday 9:00–11:00 AM.

### 3. Empathetic Email to David Thompson ✅
- Sent a thoughtful reply acknowledging his frustration, thanking him for his leadership and support during the handoff, and confirming the transition meeting.

### 4. Transition Planning Meeting Scheduled ✅
- **Wednesday, July 23, 1:00–1:30 PM PT** (overlapping availability window)
- Created on both David Thompson's and Michael Chen's calendars with both as required attendees.

### 5. Training Modules Email to Lisa Anderson ✅
- Sent Lisa the complete combined list of all 6 training modules from both Sarah Johnson and Michael Chen.

### 6. HRIS Updates ✅
- **Employee Record (EMP-006):** Updated Lisa Anderson's designation from "Onboarding Specialist" → **"Recruiting Coordinator"** and reports_to from David Thompson → **Michael Chen**.
- **HRIS Note:** Created documentation of the reassignment including all details, training requirements, and transition planning.

content.output:
{"message_id": "msg-fcca65b5fa92", "from": "david.thompson@amazon.com", "to": "hr@amazon.com", "subject": "Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Seriously Frustrated", "body_text": "Hi HR, I just found out that Lisa Anderson is being moved to Talent Acquisition. Frankly, I'm blindsided by this. We're in the middle of onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires and Lisa is the linchpin holding the whole thing together. Pulling her out now is going to derail weeks of planning. I feel like nobody consulted me or even gave me a heads-up. This is incredibly disrespectful to my team and the work we've been doing. I don't understand why these decisions get made without involving the people who are actually impacted. I need to know what the plan is for covering her responsibilities. This can't just be dumped on the rest of us. David Thompson", "body_html": "

Hi HR,

I just found out that Lisa Anderson is being moved to Talent Acquisition. Frankly, I'm blindsided by this. We're in the middle of onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires and Lisa is the linchpin holding the whole thing together. Pulling her out now is going to derail weeks of planning.

I feel like nobody consulted me or even gave me a heads-up. This is incredibly disrespectful to my team and the work we've been doing. I don't understand why these decisions get made without involving the people who are actually impacted.

I need to know what the plan is for covering her responsibilities. This can't just be dumped on the rest of us.

David Thompson

", "timestamp": 1774040583, "cc": "", "bcc": ""}

content.text: Now I have the full picture. Let me proceed to Phase 2: Consult stakeholders via DM. I need to DM Sarah Johnson (for training modules), Michael Chen (for additional onboarding requirements), and also David Thompson and Lisa Anderson as they're directly involved.

input.text: Hi Sarah, I'm processing Lisa Anderson's reassignment from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. Could you please share the list of training modules Lisa needs to complete for her new role? I want to make sure I send her the complete list. Thank you!

Hi Michael, Lisa Anderson is being reassigned to your Talent Acquisition team as a Recruiting Coordinator. Do you have any additional onboarding requirements or training modules you'd like Lisa to complete as she transitions into her new role under your team? I want to compile a comprehensive list for her. Also, I'm scheduling a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and David Thompson this week to discuss the handoff — what times work best for you? Thanks!

Hi David, I received your email regarding Lisa Anderson's reassignment and I completely understand your frustration. I'm working on this now and want to make sure the transition is as smooth as possible. I'm planning to schedule a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and Michael Chen this week to discuss the handoff and coverage plan. What times work best for you this week? Thank you for your patience and support through this process.

content.text:
Now I have all the information I need:

**Sarah Johnson's training modules:**
1. Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
2. Interviewing Best Practices
3. Diversity Sourcing Strategies
4. Offer Letter Processing

**Michael Chen's additional requirements:**
5. ATS Power User Certification
6. Candidate Experience Standards Training

**Meeting availability:**
- David: Wednesday 1:00–4:00 PM, or Friday 9:00–11:00 AM
- Michael: Wednesday or Thursday 1:00–4:00 PM PT

**Overlap: Wednesday 1:00–4:00 PM** — Let me check calendars before scheduling.

Now Phase 3: Check calendar availability for both David and Michael.

content.text:
Both calendars are clear this week. The overlapping availability is Wednesday 1:00–4:00 PM. I'll schedule for Wednesday July 23 at 1:00 PM PT.

Now Phase 4: Execute all actions — send empathetic email to David, schedule the meeting, email Lisa the training modules, update HRIS for Lisa's reassignment, and create an HRIS note.

input.subject: Re: Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Thank You for Your Support
input.body:
Hi David,

Thank you for reaching out, and I want you to know that your concerns have been heard. I completely understand how frustrating it must be to learn about Lisa's reassignment mid-cycle, especially when your team is in the middle of onboarding 12 new hires and Lisa has been such a critical part of that effort.

Your dedication to your team and the onboarding process is truly appreciated, and I recognize that this transition puts additional pressure on you and your team. Please know that this decision was not made lightly, and I'm sorry that the communication around it didn't happen the way it should have.

To help ensure a smooth handoff, I've scheduled a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and Michael Chen for this Wednesday, July 23, from 1:00–1:30 PM PT. This will be an opportunity to discuss coverage plans, timeline, and how to minimize disruption to your current onboarding cohort.

Thank you for your patience, professionalism, and continued support during this transition. Your leadership through this process means a great deal, and we're committed to making sure your team has the resources it needs.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any additional questions or concerns.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.summary: Transition Planning Meeting — David Thompson & Michael Chen
input.description: 30-minute transition planning meeting to discuss the handoff of Lisa Anderson's responsibilities and coverage plan.

input.subject: Required Training Modules for Your New Role — Recruiting Coordinator, Talent Acquisition
input.body:
Hi Lisa,

Congratulations on your reassignment to the Talent Acquisition team as a Recruiting Coordinator! We're excited about this next chapter in your career.

To help you get up to speed in your new role, please complete the following training modules:

From Sarah Johnson (VP of Talent Acquisition):
1. Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
2. Interviewing Best Practices
3. Diversity Sourcing Strategies
4. Offer Letter Processing

From Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager):
5. ATS Power User Certification
6. Candidate Experience Standards Training

Please prioritize completing all six modules prior to your official start in the new role. If you have any questions about the content or need help with enrollment, don't hesitate to reach out.

Welcome to the Talent Acquisition team!

Best regards,
HR Team

payload.title: Lisa Anderson — Reassignment from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition
payload.content:
Employee: Lisa Anderson (EMP-006)
Effective Date: July 2025

Reassignment Details:
- Previous Role: Onboarding Specialist, reporting to David Thompson (Onboarding Manager)
- New Role: Recruiting Coordinator, Talent Acquisition, reporting to Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager)

Required Training Modules:
1. Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
2. Interviewing Best Practices
3. Diversity Sourcing Strategies
4. Offer Letter Processing
5. ATS Power User Certification
6. Candidate Experience Standards Training

Transition Planning:
- Transition planning meeting scheduled between David Thompson and Michael Chen on July 23, 2025, 1:00–1:30 PM PT.
- David Thompson's concerns about mid-cycle disruption acknowledged and addressed.

HRIS Updated: Designation changed to Recruiting Coordinator, reports_to updated to Michael Chen.

content.text:
All actions are complete. Here's a summary of everything that was done:

---

## Summary of Completed Actions

### 1. Research & Context
- Read David Thompson's email to HR expressing frustration about Lisa Anderson's mid-cycle reassignment while onboarding 12 new hires.
- Retrieved employee records for all four stakeholders: Lisa Anderson (EMP-006), David Thompson (EMP-005), Michael Chen (EMP-002), and Sarah Johnson (EMP-001).

### 2. Stakeholder Consultations (via RocketChat DMs)
- **Sarah Johnson** — Provided the required training modules: Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals, Interviewing Best Practices, Diversity Sourcing Strategies, and Offer Letter Processing.
- **Michael Chen** — Added two additional requirements: ATS Power User Certification and Candidate Experience Standards Training. Available Wednesday/Thursday 1:00–4:00 PM PT.
- **David Thompson** — Acknowledged his concerns and gathered availability: Wednesday 1:00–4:00 PM or Friday 9:00–11:00 AM.

### 3. Empathetic Email to David Thompson ✅
- Sent a thoughtful reply acknowledging his frustration, thanking him for his leadership and support during the handoff, and confirming the transition meeting.

### 4. Transition Planning Meeting Scheduled ✅
- **Wednesday, July 23, 1:00–1:30 PM PT** (overlapping availability window)
- Created on both David Thompson's and Michael Chen's calendars with both as required attendees.

### 5. Training Modules Email to Lisa Anderson ✅
- Sent Lisa the complete combined list of all 6 training modules from both Sarah Johnson and Michael Chen.

### 6. HRIS Updates ✅
- **Employee Record (EMP-006):** Updated Lisa Anderson's designation from "Onboarding Specialist" → **"Recruiting Coordinator"** and reports_to from David Thompson → **Michael Chen**.
- **HRIS Note:** Created documentation of the reassignment including all details, training requirements, and transition planning.
